texto: At dusk in the Temple Gardens the barrier between past and present turned fluid and ghosts walked. Here and there if Buckler looked closely he caught a glimpse of knights filing toward the ancient round Church, heads bowed in penitence…Buckler didn’t mind the spirits. In fact, he preferred their company to that of the general run of human. For the ghosts reminded him that man’s petty cares, so all consuming in life, would one day become nothing more than fit matter for an amusing story.
explicacao: Citação de S.K. Rizzolo sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. “At dusk in the Temple Gardens the barrier between past and present turned fluid and ghosts walked…” articula reflexao, ghosts, history em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como boa-noite e reflexao-noturna.
